visibility_of_system_status.note: Health indicator, SSE badge, loading skeletons, toast notifications all present

match_between_system_and_real_world.note: Spans, traces, tokens — matches AI developer mental model exactly

user_control_and_freedom.note: Close buttons on all panels, cancel compare mode, but no undo for bulk operations

consistency_and_standards.note: Bento cards, consistent icon family (Lucide), same button/tag/tab patterns across tabs

error_prevention.note: API key confirmation for delete, but no confirm on trace export overwrite

recognition_rather_than_recall.note: Visual icons + labels for tabs, kind colors, waterfall bars are self-evident

flexibility_and_efficiency_of_use.note: Keyboard shortcuts (1-4, ?, r, d, Escape), density toggle, but no cmd+k palette

aesthetic_and_minimalist_design.note: Clean bento layout, but toolbar in trace detail is crowded with 8+ icon buttons

help_users_recognize_diagnose_recover.note: Error banner with retry, empty states with SDK guide, error panel with clickable links

help_and_documentation.note: Keyboard shortcut modal (?), SDK quick-start in empty state, but no inline tooltips

ai_slop_notes: Bento cards, Inter font, and Lucide icons are common in AI UIs but the information architecture and feature depth feel intentional and product-driven, not templated

cognitive_load.choice_overload: 5 tabs is well within limits. Toolbar has 8 icon buttons which is at the upper boundary
cognitive_load.visual_noise: Trace detail header packs 8+ data points (spans, tokens, cost, breadcrumb) — borderline
cognitive_load.memory_burden: URL persistence means no need to remember filter state. Password/API key not stored in plain sight

issues: No cmd+k command palette for power users

strengths: ARIA labels on all icon buttons
Screen reader can navigate tab structure
issues: Color alone used for span kind identification (small dot)
No reduced-motion support in animation JS (CSS only)

strengths: Feature depth — waterfall, timeline, list views, cost analytics, error tracking, comparison all in one tool
Empty state design — guides new users to SDK integration instead of blank pages
URL state persistence — filters and tabs survive refresh, enabling shareable views
Real-time updates via SSE with visual connection indicator

priority_issues.detail: Trace detail toolbar has 8+ icon buttons (compare, share, export JSON, export CSV, copy ID, close). Power users will memorize but new users face a wall of indistinguishable icons
priority_issues.suggestion: Group secondary actions into a '...' overflow menu, keep only 3-4 primary actions visible

priority_issues.detail: The small colored dot (w-1.5 h-1.5) is the only kind indicator in the list view. Color-blind users cannot distinguish flow/agent/llm/tool
priority_issues.suggestion: Add a small text label or icon alongside the color dot

priority_issues.detail: Waterfall view shows span detail below the chart. List view shows it below the list (after virtual scroll refactor). Timeline has no inline detail. Three different patterns for the same action
priority_issues.suggestion: Standardize: always show span detail in a right-side slide-out drawer

priority_issues.detail: Power users (Alex) benefit most from quick navigation. Current keyboard shortcuts (1-4 for tabs) are good but not discoverable
priority_issues.suggestion: Add cmd+k palette: 'go to costs', 'search trace #...', 'toggle dark mode'

priority_issues.detail: Detector found 4 instances of gray text on colored backgrounds (indigo-100, red-50). The gray looks washed out
priority_issues.suggestion: Use a darker variant of the background hue for text, e.g. text-indigo-700 on bg-indigo-100

minor_observations: Footer shows version but no changelog link
SearchBar has global trace search but no keyboard shortcut like cmd+/
ComparisonView '选择对比项目' label is long — could be just '对比项目'
Inter font flagged as overused — consider a more distinctive typeface for the next design iteration
provocative_questions: What if tracing-dashboard had a 'narrative mode' that tells the story of a trace execution in plain language?
Should the dashboard remember the last-viewed trace and restore it on page load (like a code editor remembers open files)?
Could the waterfall view get a 'diff mode' that compares two runs of the same flow side-by-side?
